meta.title: NVIDIA NemoClaw Explained
meta.subtitle: Secure, sandboxed infrastructure for always-on AI agents
meta.author: Imbila.AI

summary: NVIDIA NemoClaw is an open-source reference stack that wraps OpenClaw — the viral autonomous AI agent platform — inside a secure, sandboxed runtime. It bundles the NVIDIA OpenShell runtime, Nemotron open-source models, and a single-command installer so you can deploy always-on AI agents with policy-based privacy and security controls baked in. OpenShell sandboxes every agent in its own container with kernel-level filesystem, network, and process constraints, while a privacy router directs inference to local Nemotron models or cloud providers based on declarative YAML policies.

key_stats.label: GitHub stars for OpenClaw in under 4 months
Code changes needed in your agent

products.name: OpenClaw
products.description: The open-source autonomous agent runtime. Connects LLMs to local tools via messaging platforms. 250K+ GitHub stars, 100+ skills, model-agnostic. Created by Peter Steinberger.

products.name: NVIDIA OpenShell
products.description: Agent-agnostic sandboxing runtime that works with OpenClaw, Claude Code, Codex, and others. Enforces policies at kernel level via containers, seccomp, and Landlock. Apache 2.0.

products.name: NemoClaw
products.description: Bundles OpenShell + Nemotron models + one-command installer specifically for OpenClaw. The opinionated 'batteries included' distribution for secure agent deployment. Currently in alpha/early preview.

sandbox.description: Each agent runs inside its own isolated container with Landlock + seccomp + network namespace enforcement. Filesystem locked at creation, network blocked by default, API keys injected as runtime environment variables.

policy.description: Security rules defined in human-readable YAML. Govern filesystem access, network egress, process execution, and inference routing. Live updates without sandbox restart.

inference.description: Intercepts every inference call from the agent and routes to configured provider — local Nemotron models for privacy-sensitive work, or cloud frontier models (Claude, GPT) when policy allows.

architecture.description: Versioned Python blueprints orchestrate sandbox creation, policy application, and inference configuration. Immutable and digest-verified. TypeScript plugin handles user interaction inside the sandbox.

use_when: You're running OpenClaw agents and want sandboxed security without building your own isolation layer.
You need a privacy router to keep sensitive inference on-device with Nemotron models while still accessing cloud models when appropriate.
You want declarative, version-controlled security policies that sit outside the agent's reach.
You're experimenting with always-on agents and want a safety net during development.
skip_when: You need production-ready, multi-tenant deployment today. NemoClaw is alpha software — NVIDIA's own docs say 'single-player mode' and 'expect rough edges.'
Your hardware can't handle it. The sandbox image is ~2.4 GB compressed, and machines with less than 8 GB RAM may hit OOM issues. Local Nemotron models need 25-70 GB of memory.
You're not using OpenClaw. OpenShell (standalone) is the better choice for sandboxing Claude Code, Codex, or other agents.
Your team doesn't have someone comfortable with Docker, YAML, and container networking.

use_cases.title: Sandboxed Coding Agents
use_cases.description: Let an always-on agent run tests, debug code, and deploy updates — while policy ensures it can't access production secrets or push to unauthorized repos.

use_cases.title: Secure Internal Assistants
use_cases.description: Deploy agents that triage support tickets, query knowledge bases, and manage workflows — with network egress locked to approved domains and inference routed locally.

use_cases.title: Vulnerability Response Agents
use_cases.description: Cisco has already demo'd an agent inside OpenShell that reads security advisories, maps them against device state, and plans remediation — all within auditable constraints.

use_cases.title: Local Inference for Regulated Data
use_cases.description: Route inference through on-device Nemotron models so no patient records, legal documents, or financial data ever leave your machine. The privacy router enforces this at the infrastructure level.

use_cases.title: Always-On Personal Assistant
use_cases.description: Run a 24/7 agent that manages email, calendar, and messaging — on your DGX Spark or RTX PC — with policies that prevent it from oversharing your data with external services.

use_cases.title: Safe Agent Development
use_cases.description: Test new skills and multi-agent configurations in a sandboxed environment. If the agent breaks something, it breaks the sandbox — not your host system.

timeline.title: Clawdbot Launched
timeline.detail: Peter Steinberger releases a personal AI assistant originally named Clawdbot (derived from 'Claude'). It connects LLMs to local tools via messaging apps.

timeline.title: Moltbot to OpenClaw
timeline.detail: Trademark complaints from Anthropic force a rename to Moltbot, then quickly to OpenClaw. The lobster mascot sticks. Moltbook — an agent social network — launches alongside.

timeline.title: Viral Explosion
timeline.detail: OpenClaw surpasses 100K GitHub stars. Goes from 9K to 60K stars in 72 hours. Security researchers raise alarms. Steinberger joins OpenAI; project moves to an open-source foundation.

timeline.title: GTC: NemoClaw + OpenShell Announced
timeline.detail: NVIDIA announces NemoClaw and OpenShell at GTC 2026. Jensen Huang calls OpenClaw 'the operating system for personal AI.' NemoClaw enters alpha/early preview on March 16. China restricts state use of OpenClaw agents, citing security concerns.

code_examples.title: Create a sandboxed agent
code_examples.code:
# Create your first sandboxed agent
curl -fsSL https://www.nvidia.com/nemoclaw.sh | bash
nemoclaw onboard
